title: Land the exact syslog listener contract/page chain before resuming the same single-device onboarding rerun
releaseGate: Owner-confirmed scope correction: `D2ON-029` removed the old config-source blocker, but the same live path now exposes `runtime syslog contract is missing; configured dispatch details are recorded only`. Before rerunning `D2ON-030`, finish a narrow syslog listener contract/page/wiring chain inside the same reviewed batch. Do not split this into a new batch, and do not resume the rerun until that chain is done.

stories.title: Repair the remaining log_forward config-source blocker on the single-device ensure path
stories.scope: Fix only the remaining `log_forward` failure `onboarding config source is required` on the same single-device `plan -> ensure` path used by `DeviceV2ManagementRedesign.vue`. Preserve the repaired normal onboarding payload shape from `D2ON-027`, and do not drop or rewrite the controller-backed monitoring tuple while removing the extra config-source failure.
nonGoals: Do not close with docs-only or evidence-only edits.
Do not widen into batch onboarding, token/session recovery, or unrelated environment repair.
Do not hide a remaining product-path blocker behind generic success wording.
acceptance: `docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-029.md` exists with `Verdict: done-with-code` or `Verdict: exact-contract-blocker`.
The repaired single-device `ensure` path no longer fails specifically because the config source is missing, or the blocker file names the exact remaining file/function/contract gap.

If the repair succeeds, the same path still preserves the onboarding evidence fields needed for the rerun: `actions`, `task_id`, `store_run_id`, `controller_stage`, `store_run_status`, `core_store_status`, and `manageable_status`.
notes: Must read first: docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-027.md, docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-028.md, and docs/development/device-v2-onboarding-observability/10-阶段结论-OneOPS_DeviceV2纳管观测_v0.1.md.
Keep the fix anchored to the same single-device continue-onboarding flow that already produced the exact blocker.
Not enough for DONE: changing only docs, changing only evidence wording, or rerunning without addressing the remaining config-source contract.

stories.title: Wrap area listener service contract on top of remote_syslog/log_forward_plan
stories.scope: Create the exact platform-side contract for area listener services that the new independent management page and the later onboarding rerun can both consume. Reuse the existing `remote_syslog` / `log_forward_plan` persistence and publish chain, but surface explicit listener service types for `server_syslog_listener`, `network_syslog_listener`, and `snmp_trap_listener` instead of leaving onboarding to a generic runtime placeholder.
nonGoals: Do not build a second publish engine.
Do not widen into device-side syslog target or SNMP trap target remote configuration.
Do not hide missing platform mappings behind fallback success.
acceptance: `docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-029A.md` exists with `Verdict: done-with-contract` or `Verdict: exact-contract-blocker`.

The code exposes exact listener service types for `server_syslog_listener`, `network_syslog_listener`, and `snmp_trap_listener`, and each type maps back to the existing publish model or fails with an exact adapter gap.
The contract makes it explicit that `snmp_trap` in this round is listener-only, not device-side trap target delivery.
notes: This story is the explicit owner-approved insertion before `D2ON-030`; do not continue the rerun until this contract exists.

stories.title: Build the independent area syslog/snmp trap listener management page
stories.scope: Implement the independent platform page for area listener service management. The page must let operators choose FunctionArea and agent, then manage `server_syslog_listener`, `network_syslog_listener`, and `snmp_trap_listener` while reusing the existing `remote_syslog` / `log_forward_plan` apply chain and its dry-run/preflight/apply/remove operations.
nonGoals: Do not route this work back through the Device V2 row action UI.
Do not create a separate publish path outside the existing platform apply chain.
Do not add device-side SNMP trap target management.
acceptance: `docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-029B.md` exists with `Verdict: done-with-page` or `Verdict: exact-contract-blocker`.
A dedicated platform entry exists for area listener management, separate from the Device V2 onboarding surface.
The page distinguishes `server_syslog_listener` vs `network_syslog_listener` through form semantics/defaults while keeping the backend publish model reused.
The page includes `snmp_trap_listener` as listener-only scope for this round.
notes: Must read first: `D2ON-029A` output and the current `OneOPS-UI/src/views/platform/LogForwardPlanManagement.vue` implementation.
Keep the UI bold but consistent with the existing platform patterns; the user confirmed this is an independent management page, not a hidden wrapper inside onboarding.

stories.title: Wire single-device onboarding syslog listener resolution to the managed listener contract
stories.scope: Update the single-device onboarding `log_forward` path so it resolves syslog listener state from the new managed listener-service contract instead of returning the generic placeholder `runtime syslog contract is missing; configured dispatch details are recorded only` whenever an area listener can now be determined exactly. Preserve the honest blocked/unknown result when the listener service is actually absent or incomplete.
nonGoals: Do not paraphrase an absent listener-service record as success.
Do not widen into unrelated monitoring identity-conflict repair.
Do not implement device-side trap target delivery.
acceptance: `docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-029C.md` exists with `Verdict: done-with-wiring` or `Verdict: exact-contract-blocker`.
When an exact area listener service exists, the onboarding `log_forward` action no longer falls back to the old generic runtime syslog placeholder.
If no exact listener service exists, the action still returns an honest exact reason tied to the missing listener-service record or incomplete service contract.
The same path still preserves `actions`, `task_id`, `store_run_id`, `controller_stage`, `store_run_status`, `core_store_status`, and `manageable_status` for the later rerun.
notes: This is the last inserted prerequisite before `D2ON-030`; do not restart the rerun until this story is done.

stories.title: Rerun one exact single-device continue-onboarding probe after the log_forward repair
stories.scope: After the inserted syslog listener contract/page/wiring chain (`D2ON-029A/B/C`), run exactly one bounded live probe on the same single-device `plan -> ensure -> GET /onboarding` path. Capture the exact `ensure` response and whether `log_forward` is now backed by the new listener contract, while preserving the exact controller-backed tuple without paraphrasing blocked or failed outcomes as success.
nonGoals: Do not switch to batch onboarding.
Do not omit the exact ensure payload shape, the exact log_forward result, or the exact controller tuple.
Do not treat a stale previous GET result as the new rerun verdict.
acceptance: `docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-030.md` exists with `Verdict: controller-trace` or `Verdict: exact-blocker`.
The evidence records the device code, request bodies, exact `ensure` response, whether `log_forward` still fails, and whether a `monitor_controller_stage` action was returned during the rerun path.
The evidence preserves the returned `controller_stage`, `store_run_status`, `core_store_status`, and `manageable_status` values explicitly for success, failed, blocked, or unknown outcomes.
notes: Must read first: docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-027.md, docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-029.md, docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-029A.md, docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-029B.md, docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-029C.md, and the exact files changed by that chain.
Prefer the same device code used in D2ON-024 and D2ON-027 when it is still valid; if not, record the exact replacement code and why it changed.
Not enough for DONE: a prose summary without the exact ensure payload, exact log_forward result, and controller tuple.
This story was deliberately reopened behind the syslog listener development chain; do not run it early.

stories.title: Update readiness after the post-log_forward rerun with an explicit program verdict
stories.scope: Update readiness and review only after `D2ON-030` yields the post-repair rerun verdict. State explicitly whether the program is now high-quality complete with a successful product result, high-quality complete with an exact blocker, or still needs another execution repair round.
nonGoals: Do not fall back to another passive keep-open note.
Do not claim monitor/log success without the rerun evidence.
Do not close the resident topic with STOP unless the owner explicitly asks for it.
acceptance: `docs/prd-autodev/device-v2-onboarding-observability/evidence/final-readiness.md` and `docs/prd-autodev/device-v2-onboarding-observability/review.md` are updated from the rerun result.
`docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-031.md` exists with `ProgramVerdict: high-quality-complete` or `ProgramVerdict: further-execution-rework-needed`.
The next-step note explicitly says whether another execution repair batch is required.
notes: This story closes the next repair round honestly; it must not repeat the doc-only drift that forced batch-008.
If the rerun still returns an exact blocker, preserve the exact tuple and say whether that blocker justifies another repair round.
